methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Scale AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Private), post-money $29B

data.narrative:
Baykar is a private-stage defense and security company with a rumored valuation near $10B. The peer universe in Defense & Security comprises 21 same-sector entities, including 2 public comps (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers such as Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Shield AI, Saronic, and Flock Safety. However, a sufficiently tight peer-median approach is not applicable: filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier comps yields fewer than three datapoints in each category, making median-based triangulation unreliable.

Given the scarcity of narrow comparables, the analysis cannot rely on peer multiples or relative valuation. Public market data on DRSHF and IDEAFORGE.NS exists but does not provide a robust bridge to a $10B private company in the same sector, due to differences in stage, scale, and business model maturity. The named private peers (Anduril, Scale AI, Shield AI) represent the closest reference universe, though their specific valuation multiples and financials are not provided in the dataset.

The rumored $10B valuation cannot be validated or adjusted through standard triangulation methods with the available data. Key uncertainties remain: the absence of disclosed financials for Baykar, the lack of sufficient same-stage Defense & Security comparables, and limited transparency on private peer valuations. Any valuation conclusion would require either additional financial disclosure from Baykar or expansion of the comparable set beyond narrow peer filtering.
